items.title: NVIDIA GTC 2026: Vera Rubin, $1T Revenue Forecast, DLSS 5
items.summary: Jensen Huang's 3-hour GTC keynote unveiled the Vera Rubin platform, projected $1 trillion cumulative revenue through 2027, and announced DLSS 5 with AI-powered real-time rendering for gaming. Blackwell orders through 2027 already exceed $500B.

items.title: DLSS 5 — AI-Powered Real-Time Rendering Goes Photorealistic
items.summary: NVIDIA's DLSS 5 uses neural networks integrated directly into GPUs to reconstruct high-fidelity images in real time, focusing on lighting, materials, and photorealistic visuals. Demonstrates hardware-native AI surpassing general-purpose systems for specialized tasks.

items.title: GPT-5.4 Mini & Nano Hit OpenRouter
items.summary: OpenAI's GPT-5.4 mini and nano variants now available on OpenRouter. Optimized for speed and cost over maximum capability — significant latency improvements for agentic workflows where API call latency compounds across multi-step tasks.

items.title: Claude Gets Memory — Anthropic Rolls Out to All Users
items.summary: Anthropic rolled out persistent memory features to all Claude users, allowing context and preferences to carry across conversations. Positions Claude directly against ChatGPT's year-old memory features. Changes the user experience from 'tool' to 'assistant that knows you.'

items.title: Microsoft Quietly Pulls Back on Copilot Branding
items.summary: Microsoft cut plans for some Copilot AI features in Windows 11 and shipped others without Copilot branding, reducing AI bloat. Signals shift from 'AI everywhere' to 'AI where it actually helps' — responding to user feedback about intrusive AI prompts.

items.title: Grok 4.20 — Four-Agent Parallel Architecture
items.summary: xAI's Grok 4.20 uses a unique four-agent parallel architecture: Grok coordinates, Harper handles fact-checking with real-time X data, Benjamin manages logic/coding, Lucas covers creative reasoning. 128K token context, 8K output limit, native image understanding.

items.title: Tether Fine-Tunes 1B Model on Smartphone in Under 2 Hours
items.summary: Tether demonstrated a technique reducing VRAM requirements for fine-tuning 1B parameter models by ~80%, enabling fine-tuning on a smartphone in under 2 hours. Demo-stage but points toward AI customization moving from data centers to consumer hardware.

items.title: Hume AI Drops tada-1b and tada-3b-ml TTS Models
items.summary: Hume AI uploaded two new text-to-speech models to Hugging Face: tada-1b and tada-3b-ml (multilingual). Specializes in emotionally intelligent, human-sounding voice generation. Worth testing for voice generation workflows.

items.title: 271+ Model Releases Tracked in Q1 2026
items.summary: AI industry pace: roughly 3 new models released every day in Q1 2026. Trends include reasoning models trading speed for accuracy, multimodal becoming standard, and efficiency improvements delivering GPT-4-level performance at dramatically lower costs.

items.title: NVIDIA Bets Big on AI Inference — $1T Opportunity
items.summary: NVIDIA pivoting focus from training dominance to inference computing, where it faces competition from Google TPUs, Intel, and AMD CPUs. Jensen Huang called inference 'the next big wave' with a $1 trillion chip revenue opportunity through 2027.

items.title: Jensen Huang: 'AI Is a 5-Layer Cake'
items.summary: NVIDIA CEO argues AI depends on five layers that must scale together: energy, chips, infrastructure, models, and applications. Framing underpins GTC announcements and the company's strategy to remain central to the AI ecosystem buildout.

items.title: The Pace of 2026: A New Model Every 72 Hours
items.summary: Community sentiment captures the AI release pace: it feels like a significant new model drops every 72 hours. The industry is releasing at a rate that makes it genuinely difficult to keep up. Most releases are specialized models, fine-tunes, and domain-specific tools rather than frontier launches.
